sources.title: Mapping genomic loci implicates genes and synaptic biology in schizophrenia
sources.finding_one_line: PGC3 GWAS (~76,755 cases, 243,649 controls): common-variant associations at 287 distinct loci, 120 genes prioritized — highly polygenic, no single causal gene.

numbers_it_licenses.what_it_is: genome-wide-significant common-variant loci
numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: we report common variant associations at 287 distinct genomic loci.

numbers_it_licenses.what_it_is: genes prioritized as likely underpinning associations
numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: we identify 120 genes (106 protein-coding) that are likely to underpin associations at some of these loci.

claims_it_can_support: Highly polygenic (287 loci)
No single 'schizophrenia gene'

sources.title: Implications of normal brain development for the pathogenesis of schizophrenia
sources.finding_one_line: Foundational neurodevelopmental model: an early fixed lesion interacts silently with normal later maturation, so symptoms appear only when affected regions mature in adolescence/early adulthood.
claims_it_can_support: Neurodevelopmental hypothesis: early insult, late clinical expression

sources.title: Do antipsychotics work in people with schizophrenia? A review of outcomes and effect sizes.
sources.finding_one_line: Narrative review framing effect sizes: antipsychotics improve symptoms with a small-medium effect (~0.4 vs placebo) rising to ~0.9 for relapse prevention (NNT ~3). Framing/corroboration only.

numbers_it_licenses.what_it_is: typical acute-treatment effect size (framing, corroborates Huhn/Leucht)
numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: positive psychotic symptoms and global psychopathology improve with a small-medium effect size of about 0.4 versus placebo.

claims_it_can_support: Antipsychotics have a small-medium effect comparable to many general-medicine drugs (framing only)
sources.notes: 2025 pub; T3 framing — not sole support for a primary number.

sources.title: What is Minimally Adequate Treatment of Psychosis and Should Duration of Inadequate Treatment be a Clinical and Research Target? A Perspective and State-of-the-Art Review.
sources.finding_one_line: State-of-the-art review: antipsychotic non-adherence is common (44-56%) and repeatedly linked to worse outcomes (relapse RR up to 2.70, mortality RR up to 1.83) — reframing inadequate treatment as widespread, not a moral failing.

numbers_it_licenses.what_it_is: rates of antipsychotic non-adherence
numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: Inadequate antipsychotic treatment is common (non-adherence rates = 44%-56%), and repeatedly associated with poorer outcomes across several key patient-centered outcomes, including increased risk of relapse (relative risk (RR) up to 2.70...) and higher mortality (RR up to 1.83, n = 272 030).

claims_it_can_support: Non-adherence affects ~half of patients; a common, not exceptional, occurrence
Inadequate treatment associated with higher relapse and mortality

sources.title: Next-step treatment for schizophrenia non-responsive to antipsychotics: a systematic review and network meta-analysis.
sources.finding_one_line: Network meta-analysis of 59 RCTs on antipsychotic non-response: ECT augmentation (SMD -0.51) and combination (SMD -0.25) show very-weak, cautiously-considered benefit — ECT a narrow next-step, not first-line.

numbers_it_licenses.what_it_is: effect of ECT added to antipsychotic vs continuing same in non-responders
numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: electroconvulsive therapy (ECT; k = 5; n = 97; SMD -0.51, -0.96 to -0.06; very low) might be more efficacious than continuing the same antipsychotic.

numbers_it_licenses.what_it_is: certainty framing for ECT and combination augmentation
numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: Very weak evidence suggested efficacy of antipsychotic combination and ECT augmentation and they might be considered, but with substantial caution.

claims_it_can_support: ECT has a narrow, cautious role in antipsychotic-non-responsive cases, not routine

sources.title: Subanesthetic effects of the noncompetitive NMDA antagonist, ketamine, in humans. Psychotomimetic, perceptual, cognitive, and neuroendocrine responses
sources.finding_one_line: Landmark human challenge: subanesthetic ketamine (NMDA antagonist) produced BOTH positive and negative schizophrenia-like symptoms plus perceptual/cognitive impairments.

numbers_it_licenses.what_it_is: ketamine reproduces positive AND negative symptoms
numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: Ketamine (1) produced behaviors similar to the positive and negative symptoms of schizophrenia; (2) elicited alterations in perception; (3) impaired performance on tests of vigilance, verbal fluency, and the Wisconsin Card Sorting Test.

claims_it_can_support: NMDA antagonism reproduces full symptom spectrum incl. negative/cognitive
Dopamine-only accounts insufficient

sources.title: What are the functional consequences of neurocognitive deficits in schizophrenia?
sources.finding_one_line: Landmark review: neurocognition (esp. verbal memory, vigilance) predicts real-world outcome, whereas positive symptoms did NOT predict outcome in any study reviewed — the load-bearing tension.

numbers_it_licenses.what_it_is: positive symptoms do not predict functional outcome; cognition does
numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: psychotic symptoms were not significantly associated with outcome measures in any of the studies reviewed.

claims_it_can_support: Positive symptoms poor predictors of functioning; cognition strongest predictor

sources.title: The schizophrenogenic mother concept in American psychiatry
sources.finding_one_line: Historical analysis: the 'schizophrenogenic mother' etiology was downgraded by its own former promulgators from a 'half truth' to a psychiatric 'myth' — the theory existed and was refuted.

numbers_it_licenses.what_it_is: the theory existed and was later labeled a myth
numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: Though former promulgators have gone from calling the schizophrenogenic mother a "half truth" to a psychiatric "myth," this psychiatric concept remains a puzzle.

claims_it_can_support: MythVsFact: 'schizophrenogenic mother' was real historical concept, discredited

sources.title: Lack of insight among outpatients with schizophrenia
sources.finding_one_line: Outpatient study (n=87): ~half (49.5%) had at least moderately impaired insight and 25% severe deficits — insight is variably impaired along a spectrum, not simply absent.

numbers_it_licenses.what_it_is: proportion of stable outpatients with at least moderately impaired insight
numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: The illness insight of 43 subjects, or 49.5 percent, was at least moderately impaired.

numbers_it_licenses.what_it_is: proportion with severe insight deficits
numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: Twenty-one subjects, or 25 percent, had severe insight deficits.

claims_it_can_support: Impaired insight is common but graded/variable, not all-or-nothing

sources.title: Neurocognitive deficit in schizophrenia: a quantitative review of the evidence
sources.finding_one_line: Landmark meta-analysis (204 studies, 22 test variables): moderate-to-large deficits (d>.60) across every cognitive domain measured.

numbers_it_licenses.what_it_is: effect sizes across all 22 neurocognitive test variables
numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: Moderate to large raw effect sizes (d > .60) were obtained for all 22 neurocognitive test variables, and none of the associated confidence intervals included zero.

claims_it_can_support: Cognitive impairment spans every domain
Broadly based cognitive impairment
